titleEn: Take part in the seminar Internet and digital media in history teaching
excerptEn: As part of the Critical History project, the GRIAL group, in collaboration with EuroClio and Tallinn University, is organising a seminar entitled “Internet and digital media in history teaching” at the University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca from Wednesday 9 November to Friday 11 November…
contentEn:
As part of the Critical History project, the GRIAL group, in collaboration with EuroClio and Tallinn University, is organising a seminar entitled “Internet and digital media in history teaching” at the University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca from Wednesday 9 November to Friday 11 November 2022.

The seminar will focus on the study guide produced as part of the project for use in teacher training centres across Europe, one of the project's outputs. For further information, you can consult the seminar programme at https://iuce.usal.es/internet-and-digital-media-in-history-teaching/.

Are you interested in attending this seminar? Please fill in the registration form at https://forms.gle/Lkkd7uNmTd3oez3i6. The event will be held in person in Salamanca, and you will also be able to take part via videoconference.

The Critical History project (2020-1-EE01-KA201-077997) is carried out with the financial support of the Erasmus+ Programme of the European Union.

titleEn: Kids Media Day Spain 2022 and RTVE-USAL NJM Chair Barometer, 2022
excerptEn: 18 November 2022, University of Salamanca, Salamanca, Colegio Arzobispo Fonseca. “Kids Media Day Spain 2022 and RTVE-USAL NJM Chair Barometer, 2022” www.kidsandmedia.es www.childrenandmedia.es Friday 18 November 2022, from 9:00 to 18:00, Assembly Hall of the Colegio Arzobispo Fonseca and in a virtual streaming room on Zoom. https://bit.ly/3dcpc63 , free admission until both venues are…
contentEn:
18 November 2022, University of Salamanca, Salamanca, Colegio Arzobispo Fonseca.

“Kids Media Day Spain 2022 and RTVE-USAL NJM Chair Barometer, 2022”

www.kidsandmedia.es

www.childrenandmedia.es

Friday 18 November 2022, from 9:00 to 18:00,

Assembly Hall of the Colegio Arzobispo Fonseca and in a virtual streaming room on Zoom. https://bit.ly/3dcpc63 , free admission until both venues are full.

The University of Salamanca and the RTVE Corporation present the RTVE-USAL Chair “Niñ@s, jóvenes y medios” to research children's and young people's use and consumption of the media. Based at the OCA Consolidated Research Unit, the Audiovisual Content Observatory, www.ocausal.es, led by Dr Felix Ortega Mohedano. The Chair's objectives are:

  • To be a meeting point for the research, creative and entrepreneurial ecosystem in the communication, education, culture and technology fields of the Media Sector.
  • To be a national and international benchmark for researchers, creators and educational, cultural, communication and technology start-ups in the Media sector focused on audiovisual creation and production for children and young audiences.
  • To present annually the Audiovisual Barometer of Children and Young People in the Media sector, the “Audiovisual Barometer of Children and Young People in Spain” event, and the “Kids Media Day” event.

The Kids Media Day Spain 2022 event, www.kidsandmedia.es , first edition on 18 November 2022 in Salamanca, in-person and virtual, Assembly Hall of the Colegio Arzobispo Fonseca, University of Salamanca, from 9:00 to 18:00, access to the Zoom room at https://bit.ly/3dcpc63  free admission until both venues are full.

An event aimed at researchers, creators and educational, cultural, communication and technology start-ups in the Media sector focused on audiovisual creation and production for children and young audiences.

See the full programme here and the poster for the event here.

contentEn:
On 29 June 2022 the E-SPHAERA GROUP, promoted by the Vice-Rectorate for Quality and Undergraduate Studies and the Vice-Rectorate for Postgraduate Studies and University-Specific Programmes, will hold the 2nd CasUSAL Conversations.

After the opening ceremony (11:30), led by the Vice-Rectors for Teaching, there will be a lecture by Professor Faraón Llorens Largo (University of Alicante). In the afternoon, several USAL lecturers will share a range of teaching experiences.

The venue will be the Assembly Hall and classrooms of the Faculty of Translation and Documentation; registration is available until the day before, or until capacity is reached.

Access the registration form: https://forms.gle/8pqzqkNf3WNUtfeg6

contentEn:
On 8 June, taking advantage of the stay in Salamanca of Mr Laurent Paquereau and Ms Marigel García Lozano, pedagogical advisers and training engineers of the Department of the Faculty of Law and Social Sciences of the University of Poitiers and the Faculty of Arts and Languages (France), the following lectures will be given in room 17A of the IUCE:

11.00:  The Pedagolab: the place of teacher training within the University of Poitiers' pedagogical support service.

As the room's capacity is limited to 24 places, if you are interested in attending, please complete the following registration: https://forms.gle/2RFo9PPx1G8yQYFXA

A certificate of attendance will be provided.

titleEn: “Gender Equality in University Education in STEM” course
excerptEn: In this context, the consortium of the European W-STEM project, with the support of UNESCO and the Columbus Association, has designed the course “Gender Equality in University Education in STEM”, which aims to offer participants the opportunity to improve their knowledge and skills to address gender inequalities in STEM programmes and disciplines…
contentEn:
Internationally, women's participation rates in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) programmes are substantially and systematically lower than men's. Within universities, women are under-represented relative to men in positions of responsibility in STEM departments. Higher education needs to address the gender gap in these disciplinary areas by encouraging more women to take part in STEM programmes through actions to attract, provide access for, retain and guide women into these programmes.

In this context, the consortium of the European W-STEM project, with the support of UNESCO and the Columbus Association, has designed the course “Gender Equality in University Education in STEM”, which aims to offer participants the opportunity to improve their knowledge and skills to address gender inequalities in STEM programmes and disciplines in higher education. The course is aimed at teachers, researchers and decision-makers from higher education institutions involved in STEM programmes.

By taking this course, participants will understand the national and international context of gender inequality in STEM, learn how to lead and enact change at their university (or higher education institution) — including defining gender action plans and the importance of securing the support of the university leadership — and specifically address issues related to attracting, providing access for and guiding women students in STEM programmes.

The training will take place in an online format from 9 May to 5 June 2022. The course offers 40 places, registration is completely free of charge, and those who pass the course will receive an official certificate from the University of Salamanca (Spain).

Pre-registration is open until 8 May at the following link https://forms.gle/CqdupBUvqBLKxSVV6. For further information https://wstemproject.eu/es/curso-w-stem/.

titleEn: Start of the Initial Training Programme for University Teaching Staff (FIPU) 2022
excerptEn: The opening session of the Initial Training Programme for University Teaching Staff (FIPU) began at 10.00. It was attended by the 16 selected lecturers, some of the mentor teachers, the coordinators of the activity and the training coordinator. The FIPU, which will conclude in September with the submission of the…
contentEn:
The opening session of the Initial Training Programme for University Teaching Staff (FIPU) began at 10.00. It was attended by the 16 selected lecturers, some of the mentor teachers, the coordinators of the activity and the training coordinator.

The FIPU, which will conclude in September with the submission of the follow-up report, comprises 85 hours of training, divided into compulsory activities, optional courses chosen by the participants and independent reflective work.

titleEn: Draw for places on the 2022 Training Plan
excerptEn: On Wednesday 23 February, the random draw to allocate places on the courses of the 2022 Teaching Training Plan was held at the IUCE secretary's office. The procedure was as follows: Each pre-registration submitted through the platform was assigned a unique personal number. A draw…
contentEn:
On Wednesday 23 February, the random draw to allocate places on the courses of the 2022 Teaching Training Plan was held at the IUCE secretary's office.

The procedure was as follows:

  1. Each pre-registration submitted through the platform was assigned a unique personal number.
  2. A random draw was held to establish the starting number for the allocation of places in all those activities where demand exceeded the number of places on offer.
  3. Places are allocated in sequential order starting from the number drawn for each activity until the places available in the initial offer are filled; the remaining pre-registered applicants are placed on a waiting list in the same order, either to cover possible withdrawals or for a possible increase in the number of places should this prove feasible.
  4. The minutes of the draw are available at the IUCE secretary's office for consultation by any interested lecturer.

The results were as follows:

titleEn: Presentation of the Training Plan and opening of registration
excerptEn: The Vice-Rectorate for Quality and Undergraduate Studies and the Vice-Rectorate for Postgraduate Studies and University-Specific Programmes, with the support of the Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), presented this morning the 2022 Teaching Training Plan for Teaching and Research Staff, which sets out the institutional proposal for initial and continuing training, aimed at continuing to…
contentEn:
The Vice-Rectorate for Quality and Undergraduate Studies and the Vice-Rectorate for Postgraduate Studies and University-Specific Programmes, with the support of the Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), presented this morning the 2022 Teaching Training Plan for Teaching and Research Staff, which sets out the institutional proposal for initial and continuing training, aimed at continuing to support the improvement of lecturers' teaching in the context of the current official degree programmes.

The University of Salamanca offers its teaching staff an updated training programme with 85 activities, 750 hours of training and nearly 2,500 places, bringing together both the demands and proposals gathered and the future trends shaping university training, as reported by the Vice-Rector for Quality and Undergraduate Studies, M.ª José Rodríguez Conde, who was accompanied by the Coordinator for Training and Teaching Innovation, Juan Pablo Hernández Ramos, and the Director of the Institute of Education Sciences, Susana Olmos Migueláñez.

Pre-registration, which opened today, will be available until Tuesday 22 February at 14.00 in the training section: https://iuce.usal.es/formacion/

titleEn: INRED. 8th Conference on Educational Innovation and Online Teaching (IN-RED 2022) at the Universitat Politècnica de València
excerptEn: On 6, 7 and 8 July 2022, the 8th Conference on Educational Innovation and Online Teaching (IN-RED 2022) will be held at the Universitat Politècnica de València. IN-RED is a forum for showcasing innovative experiences, the application of methodologies that support learning, the implementation of improvements in the classroom…, in short, all those experiences…
contentEn:
On 6, 7 and 8 July 2022, the 8th Conference on Educational Innovation and Online Teaching (IN-RED 2022) will be held at the Universitat Politècnica de València.

IN-RED is a forum for showcasing innovative experiences, the application of methodologies that support learning, the implementation of improvements in the classroom…, in short, all those experiences related, directly or indirectly, to the quality of the teaching and learning processes carried out in higher education.

As in previous editions, submissions will be assessed on the basis of FULL PAPERS. To submit work, authors must follow the instructions set out in the CALL FOR PAPERS section, available on the conference website for the submission of papers.

IN-RED addresses topical issues in higher education, such as:

  • High-impact educational practices
  • Learning-oriented assessment
  • Integration of the SDGs in higher education
  • Active teaching and learning methodologies
  • Technological resources for quality learning

Please bear in mind the following key dates in the Call for Papers:

  • Deadline for submissions: 28-3-2022
  • Notification of acceptance or rejection of papers: 16-5-2022
  • Author registration. Once a paper has been accepted, at least one of the authors must be registered for the conference before 1-7-2022.

You will find further information about the conference (objectives, areas of interest, submission of papers, etc.) on the conference website at http://inred.blogs.upv.es, where information on the final programme will be added.

You can also contact the organisers by email at inred@upv.es.

titleEn: Audiovisual Content Observatory members Juan-José Igartua and Magdalena Wojcieszak receive the article of the year award from the journal Mass Communication and Society
excerptEn: The article of the year award of the journal Mass Communication and Society (Mass Communication & Society Division) has been given to the article “How to enhance the effects of mediated intergroup contact? Evidence from four countries”, written by Juan-José Igartua and Magdalena Wojcieszak, both members of the Audiovisual Content Observatory of the University of…
contentEn:
The article of the year award of the journal Mass Communication and Society (Mass Communication & Society Division) has been given to the article “How to enhance the effects of mediated intergroup contact? Evidence from four countries”, written by Juan-José Igartua and Magdalena Wojcieszak, both members of the Audiovisual Content Observatory of the University of Salamanca, together with Nuri Kim. The journal Mass Communication and Society is indexed in JCR-WOS Q2 (in the Communication category).

The article presents the results of four online experiments conducted in Spain, the Netherlands, the United Kingdom and Singapore, examining whether encouraging people to imagine a positive intergroup encounter before reading a personal story from an outgroup member, as well as mediated contact with an outgroup member who is similar to or different from the ingroup prototype, improves attitudes towards the outgroup. The results show that, although imagined contact and similarity do not consistently improve outgroup attitudes, greater interest in the story of an outgroup member and positive emotions mediate the effects of similarity and, in two countries, of imagined contact.

This research forms part of the project “Narrative tools to reduce prejudice. Effects of similarity, imagined contact, empathy and narrative voice” (HENAR), funded by the State Programme for the Promotion of Scientific and Technical Research of Excellence of the Ministry of Science and Innovation (2016-2020), led by Juan-José Igartua Perosanz (Ref. CSO2015-67611-P).

Article reference:

Wojcieszak, M., Kim, N., & Igartua, J. J. (2020). How to enhance the effects of mediated intergroup contact? Evidence from four countries. Mass Communication & Society, 23(1), 71-106.

Available at:

Link to the source of this news item: https://www.ocausal.es/juan-jose-igartua-y-magdalena-wojcieszak-miembros-del-oca-han-obtenido-el-premio-al-articulo-del-ano-en-la-revista-mass-communication-and-society/

Contact: Dr Juan José Igartua. Director of the Audiovisual Content Observatory. Email: jigartua@usal.es. Telephone: 676.161.042

titleEn: Virtual workshop “Post-Pandemic Educational Research: Reflections and Challenges”
excerptEn: With the aim of bringing the keynote lectures and round tables of the International Conference of Research in Education (IRED´21) closer to the teaching staff of the University of Salamanca, the IUCE is offering the chance to take part in the virtual workshop: Post-pandemic research: reflections and challenges. IRED´21 is organised by the Network of…
contentEn:
With the aim of bringing the keynote lectures and round tables of the International Conference of Research in Education (IRED´21) closer to the teaching staff of the University of Salamanca, the IUCE is offering the chance to take part in the virtual workshop: Post-pandemic research: reflections and challenges.

IRED´21 is organised by the Network of Education Research Institutes (RIIE) and will be held at the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca from 3 to 5 November 2021.

If you wish to take part in the workshop, you can register until 23:59 on 2 November, or until all the allocated places are filled, at the following link: https://forms.gle/Xhj7mGWNwnM6CYZ59

ACTIVITY DETAILS:

FORMAT: Virtual workshop

DATES: 3-12 November 2022

DURATION: 10 hours

VENUE: Virtual

No. OF PLACES: 70

Access the activity programme: https://cutt.ly/lRAZt3r

Further information: iuce.formación@usal.es / coord.docencia@usal.es

titleEn: 3rd International Conference on Social and Productive Innovation (III-CIISP)
excerptEn: 2021 CALL of the 3rd INTERNATIONAL CONFERENCE ON SOCIAL AND PRODUCTIVE INNOVATION – III-CIISP – From 16 to 19 November 2021 Lambayeque, Peru The III-CIISP is a space for interaction and the dissemination of scientific and technological knowledge on society, the environment and economies, attracting interesting contributions nationally and worldwide…
contentEn:
2021 CALL of the 3rd INTERNATIONAL CONFERENCE ON SOCIAL AND PRODUCTIVE INNOVATION – III-CIISP –

From 16 to 19 November 2021

Lambayeque, Peru

The III-CIISP is a space for interaction and the dissemination of scientific and technological knowledge on society, the environment and economies, attracting interesting contributions nationally and worldwide through keynote lectures and presentations of the results of research work related to social and productive innovation.

OBJECTIVES

  1. To disseminate the progress and results of the scientific and technological research work of teachers, researchers and student researchers in training, at local, national and international level.
  2. To foster social and productive innovation and the development of the research lines: Social Sciences and Humanities; Health Sciences; Natural and Environmental Sciences; Agricultural Sciences; and Engineering and Technologies.

THEMATIC AREAS

A) Social innovation

A.1. Capacities and entrepreneurship for social innovation

Session Chair: Mg. Ing. Oscar Capuñay Uceda; ocapunayu@unprg.edu.pe

A.2. Innovation in the field of health

Session Chair: Dr Víctor Soto Cáceres; vsoto@unprg.edu.pe

A.3. Innovation in the field of education

Session Chair 1: Dr Jessie Bravo Jaico; jbravo@unprg.edu.pe

Session Chair 2: Dr Rosa Gonzales Llontop; rgonzalesl@unprg.edu.pe

A.4. Innovation and personal, family, workplace and community well-being

Session Chair: Dr Margarita Fanning Balarezo; mfanning@unprg.edu.pe

A.5. Innovation and the protection, improvement and conservation of the environment.

Session Chair: Dr Pedro A. Del Carpio Ramos; pdelcarpio@unprg.edu.pe

A.6. Innovation and land-use planning

Session Chair: Dr Lindon Vela Meléndez; lvela@unprg.edu.pe

A.7. Evaluation and promotion of social innovation

Session Chair: Dr Haydeé Chirinos Cuadros; hchirinos@unprg.edu.pe

A.8. Postgraduate Theses – EPG

Session Chair: Dr Luis J. Collantes Santisteban; lcollantes@unprg.edu.pe

A.9. Doctoral Theses Course:

Session Chair: Dr Eduar Vásquez Sánchez; evasquez@unprg.edu.pe

B) Productive innovation

B.1. Capacities and entrepreneurship for productive innovation

Session Chair: Mg. Yefferson Llonto Caicedo, yllonto@unprg.edu.pe

B.2. Technological research for local development.

Session Chair: Dr Freddy Asrael Paz Sifuentes; fpaz@unprg.edu.pe

B.3. Scientific and technological research and the protection of intellectual property.

Session Chair: Dr Hilda A. Del Carpio Ramos; hdelcarpio@unprg.edu.pe

B.4. Innovative national and international business and innovation in business

Session Chair: Dr Carlos Querevalú Morante; cquerevalu@unprg.edu.pe

B.5. Innovative tourism.

Session Chair: Dr Janet del Rosario Aquino Lalopú; jaquino@unprg.edu.pe

B.6. Evaluation and promotion of productive innovation

Session Chair: M. Sc. Ada P. Barturén Quispe; abarturen@unprg.edu.pe

SUBMISSION OF CONTRIBUTIONS

  • Those interested should identify the thematic area of interest and request the article template and participation procedures from the Session Chair.
  • Articles and papers should be sent to the email address of the corresponding Chair and will be reviewed by two members of the Scientific Committee.
  • The Conference will be held virtually.
  • Pre-conference activities will be held.

SELECTION CRITERIA

  • The topic must be related to one of the Conference areas
  • Originality of the topic and the work (0% plagiarism and 15% similarity).
  • Quality of argumentation and grounding.
  • Relevance to human, institutional, local and regional development.
  • Quality of writing.

TIMETABLE

  • July to October 2021: Call
  • Until 15 October 2021: Receipt of articles
  • Until 30 October 2021: Acceptance of articles
  • September and October 2021: Training in scientific article writing
  • 5 – 15 November 2021: Publication of the Programme of Lectures and Papers
  • 16 – 19 November 2021: Presentation of Lectures and Papers

ENQUIRIES

  • Yeraldine Castillo Quiroz; ycastilloq@unprg.edu.pe
  • Leslie Velasco Meléndez; lvelascom@unprg.edu.pe
  • Claudia Morales Figueroa; cmoralesf@unprg.edu.pe
  • Silvia Tatiana Montalvo Gastelo; smontalvog@unprg.edu.pe
  • José Carlos Pisfil Montenegro; jpisfilmon@unprg.edu.pe
  • Hilda Angelica Del Carpio Ramos; hdelcarpio@unprg.edu.pe

REGISTRATION TO ATTEND:

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfuoIu9_U-OoINUd_5MOXwwRrWtPwZFZQBuuKQGQHiMjTUMJw/viewform?vc=0&c=0&w=1&flr=0

Call available for download in PDF:

1-Convocatoria-IIICIISPDownload

titleEn: Exhibition “History and Culture of the Roma People”
excerptEn: Since Monday 11 October, the exhibition “History and Culture of the Roma People” has been on display at the University of Salamanca, specifically at the Faculty of Education. Thanks go to the University itself, the IUCE University Institute of Education Sciences and the Faculty of Education (https://twitter.com/EducacionUsal). It can be visited until 26…
contentEn: Since Monday 11 October, the exhibition “History and Culture of the Roma People” has been on display at the University of Salamanca, specifically at the Faculty of Education. Thanks go to the University itself, to the IUCE University Institute of Education Sciences and to the Faculty of Education (https://twitter.com/EducacionUsal). It can be visited until 26 October in the entrance hall of the Faculty. Raising the awareness and knowledge of future education professionals about the Roma People is essential in order to include their History and Culture in the academic curriculum.

excerptEn: The Network of Education Research Institutes is organising the International Educational Research Award, aimed at education researchers, teachers and academics, as well as educational research groups. It is offered in two categories: • International educational research award, established groups category.• International educational research…
contentEn:
The Network of Education Research Institutes is organising the International Educational Research Award, aimed at education researchers, teachers and academics, as well as educational research groups. It is offered in two categories:

• International educational research award, established groups category.
• International educational research award, young researchers category.

Objectives:

The International Educational Research Award (PIIE) aims to:
> Stimulate the design and development of educational research projects in Spain, Portugal and Latin America.
> Promote research projects in the field that show scientific rigour in their foundations and methods.
> Improve the quality of scientific knowledge in the field of education.
> Disseminate good educational research practice, in particular the use of rigorous methodologies.

Registration deadline: 10 October 2021.

Awards ceremony: Coinciding with the 2nd International Conference on Research in Education, in Salamanca from 3 to 5 November 2021.

The full rules of the award appear on the registration form:

Access the registration form – Established groups category

Access the registration form – Young researchers category

titleEn: 1st International Conference on Educational Innovation with a Gender Perspective
excerptEn: The University Institute of Education Sciences, in cooperation with the University Institute of Women's Studies of the University of La Laguna, is carrying out the project “Incorporating the gender perspective into university teaching through the training of in-service and trainee teaching staff” (Ref.19/15ATC/20), funded…
contentEn:
The University Institute of Education Sciences, in cooperation with the University Institute of Women's Studies of the University of La Laguna, is carrying out the project “Incorporating the gender perspective into university teaching through the training of in-service and trainee teaching staff” (Ref.19/15ATC/20), funded by the Institute for Women and Equal Opportunities of the Ministry of Equality.

The project addresses the incorporation of the gender perspective in order to integrate equality content into any subject in the curriculum. This approach focuses on the training of university teaching staff, both those currently in service and doctoral students, whose competences include training to become future teachers.

This activity is subdivided into a set of actions or activities whose common thread is the cross-cutting incorporation of the gender perspective into the undergraduate and master's degrees of the University of Salamanca (USAL) and the University of La Laguna (ULL) through teaching innovation. To achieve this, initial training has been developed that lays the foundations for teachers to introduce the gender perspective into one of their subjects. This training has been delivered as Open Training within the Teaching Staff Training Plan of the University of Salamanca.

In addition, the project culminates in the “1st International Conference on Educational Innovation with a Gender Perspective”. The conference is organised in conjunction with the 11th International Conference on Virtual Campuses (JICV’21) and is run by the GRIAL Research Group of the University of Salamanca. JICV’21 also has the collaboration of the University Network of Virtual Campuses, the IEEE Education Society and the Spanish Chapter of the IEEE Education Society.

The conference will be held in a hybrid format (in person and online) on 30 September 2021. The in-person format will take place at the premises of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca (Spain).

 The submission of articles is open until 1 August. The key dates to bear in mind are set out below:

  • Submission of articles:  1 August 2021
  • Acceptance of articles: 1 September 2021
  • Submission of the final paper: 15 September 2021
  • Registration deadline: 15 September 2021
  • Submission of presentation videos: 15 September 2021
  • Conference date: 30 September 2021

Further information about the project and the conference at: https://coeducacion.grial.eu/i-jornadas-internacionales-de-innovacion-educativa-con-perspectiva-de-genero/

titleEn: Deadline extended for the submission of papers to IRED'21
excerptEn: The second edition of the IRED’21 International Conference will be held at the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE – https://iuce.usal.es/) of the University of Salamanca from 3 to 5 November 2021, in a hybrid format (in person and online) owing to the uncertainty and the situation arising from the pandemic caused by…
contentEn:
The second edition of the IRED’21 International Conference will be held at the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE – https://iuce.usal.es/) of the University of Salamanca from 3 to 5 November 2021, in a hybrid format (in person and online) owing to the uncertainty and the situation arising from the pandemic caused by COVID-19.

The deadline for submitting papers to the conference has been extended until 12 September 2021.

All the information and the call for papers are available at: https://ired2021.grial.eu/llamada-a-trabajos/
